surface_verification.justification: Framework tooling change (Tier-3 pr_review gate) with no UI surface. The change is isolated to plugins/shipwright-security (verified: no cross-plugin importers of pr_review_lib / pr_review_diff_filter), so the owning plugin's full suite is the authoritative coverage: 460 passed, 3 skipped. The 'E2E' is the filter chain, dogfooded empirically on the real PR #310 diff (295,497 -> 54,211 chars, no longer truncated, 15 generated files excluded, 0 real source dropped). The pr-review.yml workflow that invokes the tool is pinned by test_pr_review_workflow_shape.py (green).

behaviors.behavior: is_generated_path matches the generated prefixes/basenames (compliance, agent_docs, changelog drops, lockfiles, state logs) and rejects real source + prefix-collisions (.shipwright/compliance-notes/)
filter_generated_paths drops generated file-sections, keeps source, and returns a sorted+deduped excluded list; a diff with no diff --git header passes through unchanged
section-path resolution handles deletions (+++ /dev/null) and additions (--- /dev/null) via the correct side
a rename that moves real source INTO a generated dir is kept (not silently dropped); a rename where BOTH ends are generated is still excluded (Codex review SHOULD-FIX)
filtering lets a diff that WOULD truncate fit under MAX_DIFF_CHARS so the review runs instead of failing closed
build_pr_meta discloses the excluded list to the model (capped at 30 + a '+N more' marker); empty when nothing excluded
render_comment discloses the exclusion to humans; no note when nothing excluded
main() end-to-end: a big generated-dominated diff is no longer truncated, the review runs (exit 0 on approve), and the comment discloses the exclusion
the fail-closed-on-truncation behavior is preserved for a genuinely large CODE diff (after filtering)
behaviors.evidence: existing test_truncation_fails_closed_needs_human still PASSED (unchanged); filter runs before truncate, so a large code diff still truncates -> block

behaviors.behavior: the OpenRouter API key is never logged on any path
behaviors.evidence: test_api_key_never_logged (pre-existing) covers the redaction invariant this change does not touch.
